etymology.description: semantic 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh") + phonetic 各(各) (gè, "each") -> arm
etymology.interpretation: The character 胳 (gē, "arm") combines the semantic component 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh") to indicate the association with the body, particularly soft tissue like an arm, with the phonetic 各(各) (gè, "each") which suggests the sound. The presence of "flesh" directly supports the anatomical meaning of 'arm'.

examples.chinese: 他把胳膊(他把胳膊)抬起来(抬起來)。
她的胳膊(她的胳膊)上有一个纹身(上有一個紋身)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de gēbo shàng yǒu yī gè wénshēn.
examples.english: There is a tattoo on her arm.

examples.chinese: 小孩的胳膊(小孩的胳膊)很细(很細)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎohái de gē bo hěn xì.
examples.english: The child's arm is very thin.
